tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(21,12): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(26,13): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(34,12): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(35,12): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(39,19): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(43,15): error TLUA100037: Expected at most 1 arguments, but a tail may pass more.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(63,16): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(89,3): error TLUA2322: Type '...string' is not assignable to type 'string, string'.
  Target requires 2 element(s) but source may have fewer.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(123,9): error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number | boolean' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
  Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(128,9): error TLUA2322: Type 'string | nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
  Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(134,9): error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'number'.
  Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua(203,24): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string | boolean' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string | nil'.
  Type 'boolean' is not assignable to type 'string'.


==== tluaVarargAdjustment.tlua (12 errors) ====
    declare function takesNumbers(...: number): void;
    declare function takesOne(a: number): void;
    declare function takesOneNilable(a: number | nil): void;
    declare function takesTwo(a: number, b: number): void;
    declare function takesTwoNilable(a: number | nil, b: number): void;
    declare function nothing(): void;
    declare function pair(): (number, string);
    declare function midRest(): (number, ...string, boolean);
    declare function optionalTail(): (number, string?);
    declare key: number;
    
    function callArguments(...: number): void
      // Tail position expands to the full pack.
      takesNumbers(...);
      takesNumbers(1, ...);
    
      // Non-tail position adjusts to a single first value -- so this is two
      // arguments, not "the pack then 1". A rest-only pack may be empty, so that
      // value's type is `number | nil`, which the first parameter rejects.
      takesTwoNilable(..., 1);
      takesTwo(..., 1);
               ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
      // Parentheses make a vararg single-valued, with that same `number | nil`, so
      // the truncation idiom only satisfies nil-accepting targets.
      takesOneNilable((...));
      takesOne((...));
                ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
      // A parenthesized call is one value too, so the second value is dropped.
      local onlyFirst: number = (pair());
    
      // A tail imposes no arity of its own: Lua neither pads nor complains. What it
      // supplies is a type question -- a value it may not have is `number | nil`.
      // So a required `number` rejects it...
      takesOne(...);
               ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
      takesTwo(...);
               ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
      // ...while anything that accepts nil takes it, with no exception needed.
      takesOneNilable(...);
      takesTwoNilable(...);
                      ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    
      // The one arity rule left: a tail that cannot contribute. Every parameter is
      // already filled, so every value it produces would be discarded (100037).
      takesOne(1, ...);
                  ~~~
!!! error TLUA100037: Expected at most 1 arguments, but a tail may pass more.
    end
    
    declare function optionalOnly(a?: number): void;
    declare function trailingOptional(a: number, b?: number): void;
    declare function leadingFixed(prefix: string, ...: number): void;
    
    function tailIntoParameters(...: number): void
      // Every parameter the tail reaches may go without a value, so each receives
      // `number | nil` -- which is exactly what makes these legal without a special
      // case for optional parameters.
      optionalOnly(...);
      trailingOptional(1, ...);
    
      // A rest parameter absorbs whatever is left, however little, so it receives
      // the element type unchanged -- no nil, no arity check.
      takesNumbers(...);
      leadingFixed("x", ...);
    
      // But a fixed parameter ahead of the rest is still a fixed parameter.
      leadingFixed(...);
                   ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'number | nil' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    end
    
    function parenthesizedOperand(...: number): void
      // `(...` opens an arrow parameter list only when a parameter continuation
      // follows the `...`. An operator after it keeps the vararg an operand of a
      // parenthesized expression, truncated to its first `number | nil` value.
      local defaulted: number = (... or 0);
      local compared: boolean = (... == 1);
    
      // The arrow forms stay arrows.
      local arrow = function(...) return (... or 0) end;
      local annotated = function(...: number) return 0 end;
    end
    
    // Return value lists follow the same rules.
    function returnTail(...: string): (string, ...string)
      return "first", ...;
    end
    
    function returnTruncated(...: string): (string | nil, string)
      return ..., "last";
    end
    
    // An unbounded tail cannot fill a required fixed return value either.
    function returnUnderfilled(...: string): (string, string)
      return ...;
      ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '...string' is not assignable to type 'string, string'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Target requires 2 element(s) but source may have fewer.
    end
    
    function localValueLists(...: number): void
      // Tail expansion: both names come out of the pack.
      local a, b = ...;
    
      // A non-tail `...` contributes one value; the tail still expands.
      local c, d = ..., 1;
    
      // Fixed value first, tail expands into the rest.
      local e, f = 1, ...;
    end
    
    function tableConstructors(...: number): void
      // The last positional entry expands, so the tail lands in the number index.
      local expanded = {1, ...};
    
      // A non-tail `...` stores only its first value.
      local truncated = {..., 1};
    
      // A void pack in tail position contributes zero values, so this table is
      // empty -- it is not `{ 1: void }`.
      local voided = {nothing()};
    
      // Calls expand in the tail exactly like varargs.
      local fromCall = {1, pair()};
    end
    
    function tableTailShapes(...: number): void
      // A fixed element *after* an unbounded one has no static index -- it sits
      // wherever the rest ends -- so it joins the number index type instead of
      // claiming index 2.
      local mid = {midRest()};
      local notBoolean: boolean = mid[2];
            ~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number | boolean' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
    
      // An optional pack element may be absent, so the property it produces is
      // optional and its type carries nil.
      local opt = {optionalTail()};
      local maybeMissing: string = opt[2];
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string | nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type 'nil' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    
      // A computed number key and a pack tail share the number index domain: one
      // signature covers both, so neither hides the other.
      local mixed = { [key] = "s", ... };
      local both: string | number = mixed[1];
      local notJustNumber: number = mixed[1];
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'number'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    end
    
    // A union of packs is only reachable by INFERENCE. The annotation form does not
    // parse -- a parenthesized return list is committed to before the `|` is seen --
    // so writing `(number, ...number) | string` here would produce two parse errors
    // and a plain pack, quietly testing the opposite of what this section claims.
    function inferredUnionOfPacks(b: boolean, ...: number)
      if b then
        return 1, ...;
      end
      return "a";
    end
    
    function unionTail(b: boolean): void
      // A union of packs has no single positional shape, so the tail stays one value
      // rather than expanding -- and must not be READ as a pack: TargetTupleType() on
      // a union is a nil dereference, which is what used to crash the checker.
      local t = {inferredUnionOfPacks(b, 1)};
      local single: string | number = t[1];
    end
    
    function varargOperands(...: number): void
      // `...` is an operand like any other: Lua allows it under a unary operator,
      // and a parenthesized cast is how you narrow the adjusted value.
      local negated = not ...;
      local defaulted: number = (... or 0);
      local cast = (... as number);
    end
    
    // A pack tail supplies a *type* per parameter position, and every consumer --
    // arity, inference and applicability -- must agree on what that type is. These
    // pin the three that used to disagree.
    
    declare function id<T>(x: T): T;
    declare function bothOf<T>(a: T, b: T): T;
    
    function tailIntoGeneric(...: number): void
      // Inference must see what the applicability check sees. Inferring from the
      // element type alone would fix T to `number` and then reject the `number | nil`
      // the tail actually supplies -- making every generic callee uncallable with the
      // canonical Lua forward.
      local inferred: number | nil = id(...);
      local pairwise: number | nil = bothOf(...);
      // A parenthesized tail is exactly one value, so it infers the same way.
      local truncated: number | nil = id((...));
    end
    
    declare function takesPair(x: number, y: string): void;
    
    type PrefixedVararg = (a: number, b: string, ...: boolean) => void;
    
    // A contextually-typed bare `...` takes its pack from the contextual signature,
    // so here the pack has a FIXED PREFIX: `(number, string, ...boolean)`.
    local tailWithFixedPrefix: PrefixedVararg = function(...)
      // The prefix fills both of takesPair's parameters, so the tail is not "unable
      // to contribute" and there is no arity error: only the *variable* part of the
      // pack is discarded. Counting the prefix as filled-by-a-fixed-argument used to
      // report 100037 here and mask any real type error underneath it.
      return takesPair(...);
    end;
    
    function midRestIntoParameters(): void
      // `(number, ...string, boolean)` always yields at least two values, so the
      // second parameter can never go without one: it receives `string | boolean`,
      // not `string | boolean | nil`.
      takesNumberAndEither(midRest());
      // ...and the trailing fixed element is really checked: a `boolean` can land on
      // the second parameter when the string run is empty.
      takesNumberAndString(midRest());
                           ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string | boolean' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string | nil'.
!!! error TLUA2345:   Type 'boolean' is not assignable to type 'string'.
    end
    
    declare function takesNumberAndEither(a: number, b: string | boolean): void;
    declare function takesNumberAndString(a: number, b: string | nil): void;
    
    function voidTail(): void
      // A call that returns nothing contributes zero values in tail position, the
      // same rule `{nothing()}` and `local a, b = nothing()` already follow.
      takesNothing(nothing());
      takesNumbers(nothing());
    end
    
    declare function takesNothing(): void;
